text: Developing a mobile app involves several key decisions. First, you must focus on responsive design so the app looks and works well on different screen sizes. Then, choose between native development—building separate apps for iOS and Android—or cross-platform tools like React Native or Flutter, which let you write one codebase for both systems. Native apps often offer better performance and deeper integration with device features, but require more time and cost. Once built, you submit your app to official app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store for review. Finally, test thoroughly for performance to ensure fast loading and smooth navigation, as users expect a seamless experience.
